rationale: The citing paper centers on mechanisms behind online review polarity and distortion of rating informativeness, and the cited paper provides directly related evidence that a specific reviewer subset (nonconfirmed purchasers) produces systematically lower ratings and demand effects. This conceptual overlap (review valence distortion, reviewer-type self-selection/deception) makes the citation plausibly substantive rather than incidental. With a confirmed DOI match and no evidence it is just a passing mention, this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
